017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.management.logging;
018
019import java.util.*;
020
021import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.management.*;
022import org.apache.logging.log4j.*;
023import org.apache.logging.log4j.core.*;
024import org.apache.logging.log4j.core.config.*;
025
026/**
027 * {@link LogBackend} adapter for <a class="doclink" href="https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/">Apache Log4j 2</a>,
028 * for use behind the {@code /loggers} management endpoint.
029 *
030 * <p>
031 * Drives the concrete Log4j2 {@link LoggerContext} / {@link Configuration} &mdash; <b>not</b> SLF4J the facade
032 * (an SLF4J&rarr;Log4j2 binding still routes here because the emitting backend is Log4j2). Register it explicitly:
033 *
034 * <p class='bjava'>
035 *    <ja>@Bean</ja>
036 *    <jk>public</jk> LoggersSettings loggersSettings() {
037 *       <jk>return</jk> LoggersSettings.<jsm>create</jsm>().enableWrite().backend(<jk>new</jk> Log4j2LogBackend()).build();
038 *    }
039 * </p>
040 *
041 * <h5 class='topic'>Level mapping</h5>
042 *
043 * <p>
044 * Levels are Log4j2 standard level names ({@code "TRACE"}, {@code "DEBUG"}, {@code "INFO"}, {@code "WARN"},
045 * {@code "ERROR"}, {@code "FATAL"}, {@code "OFF"}, {@code "ALL"}). Reads report each {@link LoggerConfig}'s
046 * <b>configured</b> level. Sets are process-lifetime-only ({@link Configurator#setLevel(String, Level)}); the
047 * root logger is addressed as {@code "ROOT"}.
048 *
049 * <p>
050 * Log4j2 has no "inherited / no own level" sentinel the way JUL/Logback do &mdash; every {@link LoggerConfig} in
051 * the configuration carries a concrete level &mdash; so reads return a level name (never the empty-string
052 * "inherited" marker) for configured loggers, and {@code null} for a name that has no {@code LoggerConfig}.
053 *
054 * @since 10.0.0
055 */
056public class Log4j2LogBackend implements LogBackend {
057
058   @SuppressWarnings({
059      "resource" // Shared Log4j2 LoggerContext owned by LogManager/caller - not closed here.
060   })
061   private final LoggerContext context;
062
063   /**
064    * Constructor using the current Log4j2 {@link LoggerContext}.
065    */
066   public Log4j2LogBackend() {
067      this((LoggerContext) LogManager.getContext(false));
068   }
069
070   /**
071    * Constructor with an explicit {@link LoggerContext} (for tests / multi-context setups).
072    *
073    * @param context The Log4j2 logger context. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
074    */
075   public Log4j2LogBackend(LoggerContext context) {
076      this.context = Objects.requireNonNull(context, "context");
077   }
078
079   @Override /* LogBackend */
080   public Map<String,String> getLevels() {
081      var out = new TreeMap<String,String>();
082      var cfg = context.getConfiguration();
083      // The root LoggerConfig is NOT in getLoggers() (that map holds only the named, explicitly-configured
084      // loggers) - it's reachable via getRootLogger().  Surface it under the "ROOT" key.
085      var root = cfg.getRootLogger();
086      if (root != null)
087         out.put("ROOT", root.getLevel() == null ? "" : root.getLevel().name());
088      for (var lc : cfg.getLoggers().values()) {
089         if (lc.getName().isEmpty())
090            continue;  // defensive: root already handled above
091         out.put(lc.getName(), lc.getLevel() == null ? "" : lc.getLevel().name());
092      }
093      return out;
094   }
095
096   @Override /* LogBackend */
097   public String getLevel(String name) {
098      var cfg = context.getConfiguration();
099      var resolved = resolveName(name);
100      var lc = resolved.isEmpty() ? cfg.getRootLogger() : cfg.getLoggers().get(resolved);
101      if (lc == null)
102         return null;
103      var level = lc.getLevel();
104      return level == null ? "" : level.name();
105   }
106
107   @Override /* LogBackend */
108   public void setLevel(String name, String level) {
109      var target = (name == null || name.equals("ROOT")) ? LogManager.ROOT_LOGGER_NAME : name;
110      // Null/blank clears the override back to the parent's level (Configurator treats null as "remove override").
111      Configurator.setLevel(target, level == null || level.isBlank() ? null : parse(level.trim()));
112   }
113
114   private static Level parse(String level) {
115      // Level.getLevel(name) returns null for an unknown name (Level.toLevel would silently default) - hard-fail
116      // to match the JUL backend's IllegalArgumentException contract.
117      var l = Level.getLevel(level.toUpperCase(Locale.ROOT));
118      if (l == null)
119         throw new IllegalArgumentException("Not a valid Log4j2 level: '" + level + "'.");
120      return l;
121   }
122
123   private static String resolveName(String name) {
124      return (name == null || name.equals("ROOT")) ? "" : name;
125   }
126}
